get.summary: Returns the paths and configuration of the nearest project .fmu directory
get.description:
If a project is not already attached to the session id it will be
attached after a call to this route. If one is already attached this
route will return data for the project .fmu directory again.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

404.description:
The .fmu directory was unable to be found at or above a given path, or
the requested path to create a project .fmu directory at does not exist.

examples.detail: Project .fmu directory not found. It may have been deleted.

post.summary: Returns the path and configuration of the project .fmu directory at 'path'
post.description:
Used for when a user selects a project .fmu directory in a directory not
found above the user's current working directory. Will overwrite the
project .fmu directory attached to a session if one exists. If not, it is
added to the session.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

404.description:
The .fmu directory was unable to be found at or above a given path, or
the requested path to create a project .fmu directory at does not exist.

examples.detail: Project .fmu directory not found. It may have been deleted.

409.description:
A project .fmu directory already exist at a given location, or may
possibly not be a directory, i.e. it may be a .fmu file.

examples.detail: .fmu exists at {path} but is not a directory

delete.summary: Removes a project .fmu directory from a session
delete.description:
This route simply removes (closes) a project .fmu directory from a session.
This has no other side effects on the session.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

get.summary: Returns a list of Sumo assets.
get.description:
Returns a list of the assets that have been onboarded to
the Sumo platform.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

404.description: Sumo assets file not found

422.description: Invalid file content in Sumo assets file

examples.detail: Sumo assets file contains invalid assets: {errors}
Sumo assets file is not a valid JSON: {error}

get.summary: Checks if a valid global config exists at the default location.
get.description:
Checks the global config at the default project location. If the global config
does not validate, or is not found, a failed status code is returned.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

404.description: The global config file was not found at a given location.

409.description: The project .fmu config already contains masterdata.

examples.detail: A config file with masterdata already exists in .fmu at {fmu_dir.config.path}.

422.description:
The global config file did not validate against the
GlobalConfiguration Pydantic model.

post.summary: Initializes a project .fmu directory at 'path' and returns its paths and configuration
post.description:
If a project .fmu directory is already attached to the session, this will
switch to use the newly created .fmu directory.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

404.description:
The .fmu directory was unable to be found at or above a given path, or
the requested path to create a project .fmu directory at does not exist.

examples.detail: Project .fmu directory not found. It may have been deleted.

409.description:
A project .fmu directory already exist at a given location, or may
possibly not be a directory, i.e. it may be a .fmu file.

examples.detail: .fmu exists at {path} but is not a directory

422.description: The requested path exists but is not a valid FMU project root.

examples.detail: Failed initializing .fmu directory. Initialize it from a project root containing 'ert'. Did not find: {missing_project_dirs}.

post.summary: Loads the global config into the project masterdata.
post.description:
Loads the global config into the project masterdata. If the global config does
not validate, or is not found, a failed status code is returned. The endpoint
takes an optional parameter, `path` as input: This should be given as a relative
path, relative to the project root. If provided, the global config is searched
for at this path. If not, the default project path will be used.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

404.description: The global config file was not found at a given location.

409.description: The project .fmu config already contains masterdata.

examples.detail: A config file with masterdata already exists in .fmu at {fmu_dir.config.path}.

422.description:
The global config file did not validate against the
GlobalConfiguration Pydantic model.

423.description:
The project is locked by another process and cannot be modified.
The project can still be read but write operations are blocked.

examples.detail: Project is read-only. Cannot write to project that is locked by another process.

post.summary: Attempts to acquire the project lock for editing
post.description:
Tries to upgrade the project session from read-only to editable by acquiring
the project lock. If the lock cannot be acquired the project remains read-only
and the last lock acquire error is recorded in the session.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

post.summary: Attempts to release the project lock
post.description:
Tries to release the project lock for the current session.
If the lock is not currently held, the route returns an informational message.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

post.description:
Explicitly refreshes the project lock timeout if the current session
holds the lock. This should be called when the user actively indicates they
want to continue editing (e.g., pressing an 'Edit' button in the GUI).

Returns a message indicating whether the lock was successfully refreshed.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

get.summary: Returns the lock status and lock file contents
get.description:
Returns information about the project lock including whether the current
session holds the lock and the contents of the lock file if it exists.
This is useful for debugging lock conflicts and showing users who has
the project locked.

get.summary: List cache revisions
get.description:
Returns a list of revision filenames for the resource specified by the
`resource` query parameter. The filenames are used with
GET /cache/{revision_id}.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

404.description:
The .fmu directory was unable to be found at or above a given path, or
the requested path to create a project .fmu directory at does not exist.

examples.detail: Project .fmu directory not found. It may have been deleted.

get.summary: Get cache revision content
get.description:
Retrieve the content of a specific cache revision.

The revision_id should be a filename from the list returned by GET /cache
(e.g., 20260112T143045.123456Z-a1b2c3d4.json).

The `resource` query parameter selects which resource cache to read.

Returns the parsed JSON content from the cached file in the `data` field.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

404.description: Cache revision not found for the specified resource

examples.detail: Cache revision {revision_id} not found for resource {resource}

422.description: Cache revision failed validation or not supported for the specified resource

examples.detail: Resource {relative_path} is not supported for cache operations

get.summary: Get diff between current resource and cache revision
get.description:
Compare a resource file in the current project with a cached revision.

The `resource` query parameter selects which resource to diff.
The response is a list of changes keyed by `field_path`.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

404.description: Cache revision not found for the specified resource

examples.detail: Cache revision {revision_id} not found for resource {resource}

422.description: Cache revision failed validation or not supported for the specified resource

examples.detail: Resource {relative_path} is not supported for cache operations

post.summary: Restore a resource from a cache revision
post.description:
Restore a resource from a cache revision (overwrites current resource).

The current resource state is cached before overwriting (when present).

The `resource` query parameter selects which resource to restore.

**Example flow:**

1. Current resource is in state A
2. Call restore with a revision from state B
3. Your current state A is cached (when present)
4. Resource is now in state B
5. To undo: restore from the newly created cache entry (your state A backup)

401.description: No active or valid session was found

examples.detail: Cache revision {revision_id} not found for resource {resource}

422.description: Cache revision failed validation or not supported for the specified resource

examples.detail: Resource {relative_path} is not supported for cache operations

get.summary: Returns internal mappings for a specific mapping type and source system.
get.description:
Retrieves internal mappings for the specified mapping_type and source_system
from the project's .fmu directory.

Example: GET /project/mappings/stratigraphy/rms returns the
internal stratigraphy mappings whose source system is RMS.

200.description: Mappings for the requested mapping type.

400.description: Invalid mapping data or unsupported mapping type

examples.detail: Mapping type '{mapping_type}' is not yet supported

examples.detail: Mappings could not be loaded because the project contains invalid saved mappings.

put.summary: Updates internal mappings for a specific mapping type and source system
put.description:
Replaces internal mappings for the specified mapping_type and source system in
the project's .fmu directory.

The request body should contain the internal mappings collection for the
specified source system.

Example: PUT /project/mappings/stratigraphy/rms replaces the stored
internal stratigraphy mappings whose source system is RMS.

examples.detail: Mapping type '{mapping_type}' is not yet supported

get.description:
The user configuration can store API subscription keys or tokens. These are

patch.summary: Saves an API key/token to the user .fmu configuration
patch.description:
Currently only known API's can be saved to the user .fmu configuration.
Arbitrary API key-value pairs cannot be saved. The currently known APIs are:

smda_subscription

400.description:
Occurs when trying to save a key to an unknown API. An API is unknown
if it is not a predefined field in the fmu-settings UserAPIKeys model.

examples.detail: API id {api_key.id} is not known or supported

401.description: No active or valid session was found

403.description: The OS returned a permissions error while locating or creating .fmu

examples.detail: Permission denied loading user .fmu config at {config.path}

404.description:
The .fmu directory was unable to be found at or above a given path, or
the requested path to create a project .fmu directory at does not exist.

examples.detail: User .fmu config at {config.path} does not exist

post.description:
When creating a session the application will ensure that the user
.fmu directory exists by creating it if it does not.

If a valid session already exists when POSTing to this route, the
existing session will be renewed with a new expiry date and a new
session cookie value.

After creating the session, the application will attempt to find the
nearest project .fmu directory above the current working directory and
add it to the session if found. If not found, no project will be
associated. Renewing an existing session keeps its current state.

The session cookie set by this route is required for all other
routes. Sessions are not persisted when the API is shut down.

401.description:
Occurs when no token or an invalid token is or is not provided
with the x-fmu-settings-api header.

403.description:
Will occur if the operating system claims the user does not have
permission to create $HOME/.fmu. If returned something very wrong
is happening.

409.description:
Occurs when trying to create a user .fmu directory, but it already
exists. Typically means that .fmu exists as a file.

examples.detail: User .fmu already exists but is invalid (i.e. is not a directory)

get.summary: Fetches the current session state
get.description: Retrieves the latest session metadata.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

session. The token applied via this route is typically a dependency for

400.description:
Occurs when trying to save a key to an unknown access scope. An
access scope/token is unknown if it is not a predefined field in the
the session manager's 'AccessTokens' model.

examples.detail: Access token id {access_token.id} is not known or supported

post.summary: Open an RMS project and store it in the session
post.description:
Open an RMS project and store it in the session.

The RMS project path must be configured in the project's .fmu config file.
Once opened, the project remains open in the session until explicitly closed
or the session expires. This allows for efficient repeated access without
reopening the project each time.

The endpoint takes an optional parameter, `rms_version`, as input. This can be
used to specify which version of RMS API that should be used when opening
the RMS project.

400.description: RMS project path is not configured in the project config file.

examples.detail: RMS project path is not set in the project config file.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

404.description: RMS project was not found or its version could not be determined.

examples.detail: RMS project does not exist at the configured path: {rms_project_path}.
RMS version cannot be determined because the RMS project .master file is not found at {rms_project_path}.
Could not open project using RMS version {version}.
Unable to check out required license.
Failed setting up RMS API proxy: The requested RMS version {version} is not supported. Try specifying another RMS version or upgrade the RMS project.

delete.summary: Close the RMS project in the session
delete.description:
Close the RMS project that is currently open in the session.

This removes the RMS project reference from the session. The project
should be closed when it is no longer needed to free up resources.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

get.summary: Checks whether or not the current session is capable of querying SMDA
get.description:
A route to check whether the client is capable of querying SMDA APIs
with their current session. The requirements for querying the SMDA API via
this API are:

1. A valid session
2. An SMDA subscription key in the user's .fmu API key configuration
3. A valid SMDA access token scoped to SMDA's user_impersonation scope

A successful response from this route indicates that all other routes on the
SMDA router can be used.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

503.description: SMDA API is unreachable or returns an error

post.summary: Searches for a field identifier in SMDA
post.description:
A route to search SMDA for a field (asset) by its named identifier.

This endpoint applies a projection to the SMDA query so that only the relevant
data is returned: an identifier known by SMDA, its corresponding UUID, and the
main country the field belongs to, not a list of countries where the field
exists. The UUID should be used by other endpoints required the collection of
data by a field, i.e. this route is a dependency for most other routes.

The number of results (hits) and number of pages those results span over is also
returned in the result. This endpoint does not implement pagination. The
current expectation is that a user would refine their search rather than page
through different results.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

examples.detail: Malformed response from SMDA: no 'data' field present

503.description: An API call to SMDA times out or fails

examples.detail: SMDA API request timed out. Please try again.

post.summary: Retrieves masterdata for fields to be confirmed in the GUI
post.description:
A route to gather prospective SMDA masterdata relevant to FMU.

This route receives a list of valid field names and returns masterdata that
pertains to them. The field names should be valid as returned from the
`smda/field` route. Callers may also provide a field UUID to disambiguate
fields that share the same identifier. If the first field does not include
a UUID, the endpoint searches for all provided field identifiers. If the
first field includes a UUID, the endpoint looks up only that field, even if
more fields are included in the request.

The data returned from this endpoint is meant to be confirmed by the user who
may need to do some additional selection or pruning based upon the model they
are working from.

One example of this is changing the coordinate system. A model may use a
coordinate system different from the one set as the field's default coordinate
system in SMDA. To match the way this works on SMDA, every coordinate system
known to SMDA is returned.

This endpoint does multiple calls to the SMDA API, any of which may possibly
fail. In any of these calls fails incomplete data will _not_ be returned; that
is, a successful code with partial data will not be returned.

400.description: Invalid parameters are provided to SMDA API

examples.detail: At least one SMDA field must be provided
A stratigraphic column identifier must be provided

401.description: No active or valid session was found

404.description: A requested resource is not found in SMDA

422.description:
SMDA returns valid data but it doesn't meet expected criteria,
or no results are found for a valid query.

examples.detail: No stratigraphic units found for column: {identifier}

500.description:
SMDA returns a malformed response that doesn't match
the expected structure.

examples.detail: Malformed response from SMDA: no 'data' field present

503.description: An API call to SMDA times out or the service is unavailable.

examples.detail: SMDA API request timed out. Please try again.

post.summary: Retrieves stratigraphic units for a stratigraphic column
post.description:
A route to gather stratigraphic units from SMDA for a specified
stratigraphic column.

This route receives a valid stratigraphic column identifier
and returns stratigraphic units associated with it. The identifier
should be obtained from the `stratigraphic_columns` field in the
`smda/masterdata` response.

400.description: Invalid parameters are provided to SMDA API

examples.detail: At least one SMDA field must be provided
A stratigraphic column identifier must be provided

401.description: No active or valid session was found

404.description: A requested resource is not found in SMDA

422.description:
SMDA returns valid data but it doesn't meet expected criteria,
or no results are found for a valid query.

examples.detail: No stratigraphic units found for column: {identifier}

500.description:
SMDA returns a malformed response that doesn't match
the expected structure.

examples.detail: Malformed response from SMDA: no 'data' field present

503.description: An API call to SMDA times out or the service is unavailable.

examples.detail: SMDA API request timed out. Please try again.

post.summary: Match source names to target names
post.description:
Match source names to target names using strict name similarity.

The endpoint is a pure matching utility. Callers provide both the
source names and target names, and can optionally provide string
replacement rules to apply before matching.

Names are normalized before matching by lowercasing, replacing
underscores, dots, dashes, and slashes with spaces, collapsing
whitespace, and applying optional replacement rules. Replacement
rules match whole normalized token sequences only, so a rule like
`Fm -> Formation` changes `Tarbert Fm` to `Tarbert Formation`,
while `Top -> ""` leaves `Stop Viking` unchanged.

The response contains one result per source. Each result contains up
to three target matches, ordered from highest to lowest score.

Confidence levels:
- 'high': score > 80
- 'medium': score 50-80
- 'low': score < 50

422.description: The request body is missing required match input or contains invalid data.

examples.detail: Input should be a valid list

get.summary: A health check on the /v1 routes.
get.description: This route requires a valid session to return 200 OK. it can used to check if the user has a valid session.

401.description: No active or valid session was found

get.summary: A health check on the application
get.description: This route requires no form of authentication or authorization. It can be used to check if the application is running and responsive.

InternalStratigraphyIdentifierMapping.title: InternalStratigraphyIdentifierMapping

Stratigraphy identifier mapping stored in .fmu/mappings.json.

Use ``to_stratigraphy_mappings()`` on the collection model when consumers
need the fmu-datamodels mapping schema.

InternalStratigraphyMappings.title: InternalStratigraphyMappings
InternalStratigraphyMappings.description:
Collection of stratigraphy mappings stored in .fmu/mappings.json.

This internal model can keep same-system alias information and unmappable
relation. Converting to fmu-datamodels drops unmappable entries and expands
same-system aliases onto matching cross-system primary mappings.

InternalWellboreIdentifierMapping.title: InternalWellboreIdentifierMapping
InternalWellboreIdentifierMapping.description:
Wellbore identifier mapping stored in .fmu/mappings.json.

Use ``to_wellbore_mappings()`` on the collection model when consumers need
the fmu-datamodels mapping schema.

InternalWellboreMappings.title: InternalWellboreMappings
InternalWellboreMappings.description:
Collection of wellbore mappings stored in .fmu/mappings.json.

This internal model can keep same-system alias information and unmappable
relation. Converting to fmu-datamodels drops unmappable entries and expands
same-system aliases onto matching cross-system primary mappings.

.. note::
   Synonyms for "model" in this context are "template", "setup", etc. The term
   "model" is ultra-generic but was chosen before e.g. "template" as the latter
   deviates from daily communications and is, if possible, even more generic
   than "model".
